metadata.title: Isomorphism Graph of Equations
metadata.method: Canonical tree form = operator sequence ignoring variable names; edges = same canonical tree

largest_component.insight: One canonical tree 'DIV' unifies 7 equations across 6 domains: Electromagnetism, Geology, Astrophysics, Epidemiology, Materials Science. Representative equations: Young's modulus E=sigma/eps; Wien's displacement law lambda_max=b/T; Curie law chi=C/T. This is the most universal mathematical structure in the catalog.

most_universal_structure.cross_domain_insight: The tree 'MUL' appears in 7 equations spanning domains: Astrophysics, Biology, Classical Mechanics, Electromagnetism, Epidemiology, Materials Science. This operator pattern is the most universal mathematical structure across scientific domains.

most_connected_equations.name: Malthus recursion N(t+1)=lambda*N(t)
most_connected_equations.interpretation: Has 6 isomorphic partners across multiple scientific domains

key_findings: Most universal tree: 'MUL' with Malthus recursion N(t+1)=lambda*N(t) as prototype
The MUL_EXP_NEG_MUL tree (Arrhenius/exponential-decay) unifies Chemistry, Biology, Geology, Economics, Pharmacology
The DIV tree (1n ratio) is the minimal universal structure: Wien, Curie, subduction speed, fatality rate
The RECIP_ADD_EXP_NEG (sigmoid) tree unifies statistical mechanics (Fermi-Dirac), neural networks (sigmoid), and ecology (logistic growth)
The MUL_POW (power law) tree unifies allometric scaling, fatigue, adsorption isotherms
Van't Hoff and Clausius-Clapeyron are perfectly isomorphic — thermodynamic universality
Doubling time, half-life, and prime counting approximation share the same DIV_LN tree
